title: SELECT, Projection, Expressions, Types, and Result Contracts · Result-Grain Case: Scalar Expressions
summary: An operations analyst needs a readable, reproducible ticket extract whose columns carry explicit meaning. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql scalar expressions increment in a typed ticket projection query pack. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build arithmetic, text, date, and CASE expressions while tracing input and output meaning.
Use relation and column aliases to make provenance and output contracts unambiguous.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 4, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp. Competency probe: Build arithmetic, text, date, and CASE expressions while tracing input and output meaning. A defensible solution applies Scalar Expressions, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a typed ticket projection query pack.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-scalar-expressions — Build arithmetic, text, date, and CASE expressions while tracing input and output meaning.
SELECT ticket_id,
       COALESCE(CAST(owner_id AS TEXT), 'unassigned') AS owner,
       CASE severity WHEN 'critical' THEN 1 WHEN 'high' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END AS severity_order
FROM tickets
WHERE resolved_at IS NULL
ORDER BY severity_order, opened_at, ticket_id
LIMIT 7;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
